danger_signals.visual_form: hostile creatures approach party visually in 3D space; melee/ranged attacks trigger on player approach
danger_signals.meaning: immediate combat threat; danger escalates with creature type variety

danger_signals.visual_form: character status indicators in upper left of UI show health point reduction
danger_signals.meaning: party vulnerability; death risk if health reaches zero

danger_signals.visual_form: numerous monsters and enemies of varying types visually distinct in 3D environment
danger_signals.meaning: threat complexity increases; different enemy types require different tactical responses

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC merchants in game world offer equipment purchases
safety_signals.meaning: safe trading zones; opportunity to acquire defensive gear and supplies

safety_signals.visual_form: usable items (potions) found by killing enemies, breaking containers, or vendor purchase
safety_signals.meaning: health/mana restoration capability; party recovery and sustainability options

safety_signals.visual_form: player-controlled formation positioning and aggression levels of AI-controlled party members
safety_signals.meaning: tactical autonomy and defensive positioning; risk mitigation through group coordination

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: race of creatures with distinct visual design as invading force
another race resurgent after 300-year imprisonment; visually distinguishable hostile entities

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: pseudo-medieval kingdom of Ehb; towns and settlements represent player-aligned territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Krug creatures present throughout game world as hostile encounters; seamless continuous environment filled with enemy patrols
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: creatures attack whenever party approaches; multiple enemy types demonstrate organized incursion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Seck race enemies appear as distinct visual entities different from Krug; environmental presence indicates trapped threat now released
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Seck entities engage in combat encounters; pose escalated threat requiring defeat as primary objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: single large seamless world without loading screens; terrain and environment change as player journeys through kingdom
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: party moves through largely linear world; enemy encounters scale in complexity as progression advances

environmental_storytelling.example: seamless pseudo-medieval kingdom presented as single continuous area without level breaks; allows player immersion in persistent world state showing invasion's scope
numerous monsters and enemies of varying types encountered throughout environment; visual diversity and placement suggests different invasion forces and threat zones
nearby towns and settlements exist within game world; player initially seeks to warn town of invasion, establishing civilian stakes and safe zone contrast with monster-filled wilderness
Goblin mines (evident from screenshots showing eight-person party fighting robots in mine environment); specialized locations with themed enemy types indicate territorial occupation by invaders

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: AI-controlled characters move in formation relative to player-controlled character; aggression level adjustable by player to control engagement risk
monsters and enemies attack whenever party approaches; behavior triggers on proximity, indicating active threat posture
using weapons or magic of particular type increases skill with that weapon over time; visible stat gains (strength, dexterity, intelligence) reflect combat experience
player controls overall tactics and weapons/magic usage of party; primary character's actions direct group behavior and formation adjustments

notes: Dungeon Siege uses minimal visual factions but distinguishes enemy races (Krug, Seck) through creature design. World-state communication relies heavily on seamless environmental continuity and enemy distribution across pseudo-medieval landscape. Safety signals emerge through vendor/settlement presence and player tactical control. The absence of level boundaries creates immersion-based danger signaling—player must navigate continuous world with threat intensity varying by location and encounter type. T3 data tier limits specific visual color/symbol documentation; visual design likely differentiated through creature silhouettes and animation rather than faction heraldry.
